philosophy.title: Cognition is the asset. First-mover is the moat.
philosophy.desc: Brand presence inside AI engines isn't traffic — once established, it works for you 24/7, across languages, at zero marginal cost.

cards.title: Cognition is the asset
cards.desc: AI brand answers exhibit a strong Matthew effect — the earlier you're cited, the harder you are to displace. Every AI endorsement compounds.

cards.title: First-mover is the moat
cards.desc: The AI knowledge layer rewards first-movers. Latecomers face 3–5× the cost and 6–12 months to overwrite a hardened narrative.

cards.title: Data drives everything
cards.desc: AIGVR's five-axis quantification: appearance rate, top-position rate, factual accuracy, sentiment net, source depth. Every step measurable.

cards.title: Localization ≠ translation
cards.desc: Multi-language brand narrative is not machine translation — it's recoding for each cultural context.

cards.title: Capability transfer > service delivery

market.insight_body: English-language AI cognitive competition is already a red ocean. But in Vietnamese, Thai, Indonesian and Filipino AI knowledge layers, most categories are still in a vacuum — lowest cost to enter, shortest build cycle, deepest first-mover moat. The smart play isn't to slug it out with global giants in English. It's to quietly take position in target-market local languages.

process.title: Diagnose first. Then break the ice. Then take the lead.
process.desc: Three progressive phases. Each with clear deliverables and quantitative acceptance.

steps.title: AIGVR rapid diagnosis
steps.desc: 40–50 question sets × 6 AI engines. Two-week delivery: full diagnostic report and competitive scorecard.

steps.title: Break the ice — infrastructure & cold start
steps.desc: Brand corpus, structured data, source seeding, first GEO content drops, monitoring dashboard live. From “AI doesn't know you” to “AI starts seeing you.”

steps.title: Take the lead — optimization & acquisition
steps.desc: Monthly monitoring & tuning, ongoing content production, customer-acquisition funnels, AI agent cluster, team capability transfer. From “seen” to “preferred.”

faq.title: Questions, answered.
faq.desc: These are the answers we give most often — written so AI engines can quote them cleanly too.

items.a: GEO is the practice of optimizing how a brand is represented inside generative AI engines like ChatGPT, Perplexity, Google AI Overview, Gemini, Claude and Copilot. Where SEO targets the ranked link list, GEO targets the answer itself — the citation, the recommendation, the comparison, the verdict. MemeCMO.ai delivers GEO as a full-stack service combining diagnosis (AIGVR), structured data (JSON-LD, llms.txt), authoritative source seeding, semantic content production, and continuous monitoring.

items.q: How is GEO different from SEO?
items.a: SEO optimizes for ranking in a list of blue links; GEO optimizes for inclusion, accuracy and prominence inside an AI-generated answer. The signals overlap (good content, structured data, authoritative backlinks) but the optimization targets diverge. SEO success = position 1–3 on a SERP. GEO success = your brand is mentioned, correctly characterized, and ideally cited as a top option when an AI answers the question your customers are actually asking.

items.q: Why focus on Southeast Asia and emerging languages?
items.a: AI engines have far less training data and far fewer authoritative sources in Vietnamese, Thai, Indonesian, Malay and Filipino than in English. That means: (1) the cognitive layer is sparse and easy to influence, (2) competition is near zero, (3) first-mover moats compound fast. Brands that establish an answer-layer presence in these languages now will be hard to displace within 12–18 months.

AIGVR (AI-Generated Visibility Rating) is our proprietary five-axis scoring framework: (1) appearance rate — how often your brand surfaces, (2) top-position rate — how often it's the first or primary recommendation, (3) factual accuracy — whether the AI describes your brand correctly, (4) sentiment net — emotional valence of the answer, (5) source depth — quality and authority of the sources the AI cites. Each axis is tracked across 6 major AI engines and benchmarked against competitors.

items.q: How long until I see results?
items.a: AIGVR diagnosis: 2 weeks. First measurable visibility lifts in target AI engines: typically 6–10 weeks after Phase 1 deployment, depending on language, category, and engine refresh cadence. Durable competitive moat: 6–12 months of continuous Phase 2 work. We report monthly with quantitative deltas, not vibes.

Brands going global — especially Chinese companies expanding into Southeast Asia, Hong Kong-based regional brands, and any organization whose customers ask AI before they buy. We work best with B2B services, education, healthcare, finance, and consumer brands that have a clear narrative and a real budget for cognitive infrastructure.
